Czech mixed martial arts promotion
2023 in Oktagon MMA

First dateFebruary 11, 2023
Last dateDecember 29, 2023

Chronology
2022 in Oktagon MMA 2023 in Oktagon MMA 2024 in Oktagon MMA

The year 2023 was the 7th year in the history of the Oktagon MMA, a mixed martial arts promotion based in Czech Republic and Slovakia. 2023 began with Oktagon 39.

List of events

[edit]
# Event Date Venue Location
1 Oktagon 39: Jungwirth vs. Oliveira Feb 11, 2023 Audi Dome Munich, Germany
2 Oktagon 40: Kozma vs. Siwiec Mar 4, 2023 Ostravar Aréna Ostrava, Czech Republic

4 Oktagon 42: Keita vs. Tichota Apr 29, 2023 Ondrej Nepela Arena Bratislava, Slovakia
5 Oktagon 43: Kincl vs. Vémola 2 May 20, 2023 O2 Arena Prague, Czech Republic
6 Oktagon 44: Langer vs. Poppeck Jun 17, 2023 Rudolf Weber-Arena Oberhausen, Germany
7 Oktagon 45 Special: Mågård vs. Lima Jul 28, 2023 Central Tennis Court Štvanice Prague, Czech Republic
8 Oktagon 45: Sanikidze vs. Keita Jul 29, 2023
9 Oktagon 46: Dalisda vs. Austin Sep 16, 2023 Festhalle Frankfurt Frankfurt, Germany
10 Oktagon 47: Vémola vs. Langer Oct 7, 2023 Tipos Aréna Bratislava, Slovakia
11 Oktagon 48: Aby vs. Garcia Nov 4, 2023 AO Arena Manchester, England
12 Oktagon 49: Moeil vs. Todev Nov 18, 2023 Lanxess Arena Cologne, Germany
13 Oktagon 50: Keita vs. Samsonidse Dec 9, 2023 Ostravar Aréna Ostrava, Czech Republic
14 Oktagon 51: Tipsport Gamechanger Final Dec 29, 2023 O2 Arena Prague, Czech Republic

Oktagon 39: Jungwirth vs. Oliveira

[edit]
Oktagon 39: Jungwirth vs. Oliveira
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateFebruary 11, 2023
VenueAudi Dome
CityMunich, Germany
Attendance7,000

Oktagon 39: Jungwirth vs. Oliveira was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on February 11, 2023, in Munich, Germany.

Background

[edit]

A welterweight bout between Christian Jungwirth and Denilson Neves de Oliveira headlined the event.[1]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Welterweight Christian Jungwirth def. Denilson Neves de Oliveira Decision (split) (29–28, 28–29, 29–28) 3 5:00 [a]
Light Heavyweight Alexander Poppeck def. Martin Zawada Decision (unanimous) (30–26, 30–25, 30–25) 3 5:00
Featherweight Losene Keita def. Samuel Bark TKO (punches) 3 0:30
Light Heavyweight Pavol Langer def. Rafael Xavier Decision (split) (29–28, 27–30, 29–28) 3 5:00
Lightweight Ronald Paradeiser def. Vladimir Šikić TKO (punches) 2 2:08
Heavyweight Hatef Moeil def. Wallyson Carvalho Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Catchweight (213 lb) Vlasto Čepo vs. Héctor Lombard Draw (time limit) 3 3:00 [b]
Featherweight Vladimír Lengal def. Cem Kuyumcuoglu TKO (punches) 2 4:30
Welterweight Marek Bartl def. Kevin Hangs KO (punches) 1 2:03
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Welterweight Lukas Cruz def. Tayo Odunjo Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 29–28) 3 5:00
  1. ^ Neves missed weight (177 lb).
  2. ^ Boxing bout.

Oktagon 40: Kozma vs. Siwiec

[edit]
Oktagon 40: Kozma vs. Siwiec
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateFebruary 10, 2023
VenueOstravar Aréna
CityOstrava, Czech Republic

Oktagon 40: Kozma vs. Siwiec was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on March 4, 2023, in Ostrava, Czech Republic.

Background

[edit]

The event featured the opening rounds of Tipsport Gamechanger Tournament 1 in a welterweight divisions and headlined between former Oktagon Welterweight Champion David Kozma and Łukasz Siwiec.[2]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Welterweight David Kozma def. Łukasz Siwiec Decision (split) (29–28, 28–29, 29–28) 3 5:00 [a]
Welterweight Alex Lohoré def. Samuel Krištofič Decision (unanimous) (29–27, 29–27, 29–28) 3 5:00 [b]
Welterweight Christian Jungwirth def. Tato Primera Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00 [c]
Welterweight Louis Glismann def. Andrej Kalašnik Decision (unanimous) (30–26, 30–26, 30–27) 3 5:00 [d]
Welterweight Andreas Michailidis def. Leandro Silva Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 29–28) 3 5:00 [e]
Lightweight Jaroslav Pokorný def. Jan Široký TKO (punches) 2 3:09
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Welterweight Marcel Mohamed Grabinski def. Máté Kertész KO (punches) 1 2:50 [f]
Welterweight Bojan Veličković def. Ion Surdu Submission (anaconda choke) 1 2:55 [g]

Free Prelims (YouTube)
Welterweight Matúš Juráček def. John Palaiologos Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00 [i]

Oktagon 41: Mågård vs. Lopez

[edit]
Oktagon 41: Mågård vs. Lopez
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateApril 15, 2023
VenueHome Credit Arena
CityLiberec, Czech Republic

Oktagon 41: Mågård vs. Lopez was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on April 15, 2023, in Liberec, Czech Republic.

Background

[edit]

A Oktagon Bantamweight Championship unification bout between current champion Jonas Mågård and interim champion Gustavo Lopez headlined the event.[3]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Bantamweight Jonas Mågård (c) def. Gustavo Lopez (ic) TKO (punches) 2 0:31 [a]
Catchweight (203 lb) Karlos Vémola def. Al Matavao Submission (rear-naked choke) 1 3:18 [b]
Light Heavyweight Dan Vinni def. Daniel Škvor Submission (heel hook) 1 1:20
Heavyweight Lazar Todev def. Adam Pałasz Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Catchweight (161 lb) Jan Malach def. Ebrahim Hosseinpour TKO (elbows) 1 2:03 [c]
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Light Heavyweight Mateusz Strzelczyk def. Jorick Montagnac KO (punch) 1 2:51
Catchweight (192 lb) Václav Mikulášek def. Matyas Viszlay KO (knee to the body) 1 4:47
Catchweight (214 lb) Nermin Hajdarpašić vs. Robin Fošum Draw (split) (29–28, 28–29, 28–28) 3 5:00

Lightweight Hafeni Nafuka def. Peter Gabal Submission (guillotine choke) 2 2:42
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Catchweight (192 lb) Melvin Mané def. Szymon Broncel TKO (elbows) 2 2:20
  1. ^ For the Oktagon Bantamweight Championship.
  2. ^ Matavao missed weight (209 lb).
  3. ^ Malach missed weight (165 lb).

Oktagon 42: Keita vs. Tichota

[edit]
Oktagon 42: Keita vs. Tichota
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateApril 29, 2023
VenueZimny Stadion Ondreja Nepelu
CityBratislava, Slovakia

Oktagon 42: Keita vs. Tichota was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on April 29, 2023, in Bratislava, Slovakia.

Background

[edit]

A Oktagon Featherweight Championship bout between current champion Mate Sanikidze and current Oktagon Lightweight Champion Losene Keita was scheduled to headline the event. However, Sanikidze withdrew shoulder injury and was replaced by Jakub Tichota for the interim title.[4]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Featherweight Losene Keita def. Jakub Tichota TKO (punch) 4 3:53 [a]
Middleweight Vlasto Čepo def. Ion Taburceanu TKO (punches) 2 1:54
Lightweight Karol Ryšavý def. Matouš Kohout Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 29–28) 3 5:00
Catchweight (229 lb) Jeremy Kimball def. Ruben Wolf TKO (punches) 2 4:05
Catchweight (157 lb) Shem Rock vs. Arthur Lima Draw (majority) (29–28, 28–28, 28–28) 3 5:00
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Featherweight Niko Samsonidse def. Roman Paulus Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Heavyweight Stuart Austin def. John Winter Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Women's Strawweight Katharina Dalisda def. Isabela de Pádua TKO (elbows) 3 4:42
Featherweight Jakub Dohnal def. Callum Mullen Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Catchweight (187 lb) Marek Bartl def. Ole Magnor Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00 [b]
  1. ^ For the interim Oktagon Featherweight Championship.
  2. ^ Oktagon Underground Rules (3min / 3R).

Oktagon 43: Kincl vs. Vémola 2

[edit]
Oktagon 43: Kincl vs. Vémola 2
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateMay 20, 2023
VenueO2 Arena
CityPrague, Czech Republic

Oktagon 43: Kincl vs. Vémola 2 was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on May 20, 2023, in Prague, Czech Republic.

Background

[edit]

A Oktagon Middleweight Championship between current champion Patrik Kincl and former champion (also a current Oktagon Light Heavyweight Champion) Karlos Vémola headlined the event. While, this event also featured the two bouts quarterfinals of Tipsport Gamechanger Tournament 1 in a welterweight divisions.[5]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Middleweight Patrik Kincl (c) def. Karlos Vémola Decision (unanimous) (49–46, 50–45, 50–45) 5 5:00 [a]
Welterweight David Kozma def. Alex Lohoré Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00 [b]
Welterweight Louis Glismann def. Melvin van Suijdam Submission (reserve omoplata) 1 4:57 [c]
Middleweight Robert Bryczek def. Lee Chadwick KO (punch) 1 2:41
Lightweight Mateusz Legierski def. Matěj Kuzník Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Catchweight (175 lb) Miroslav Brož def. Robert Pukač Decision (split) (28–29, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Middleweight Marek Mazúch def. Giovanni Melillo KO (punches) 2 1:53
Catchweight (179 lb) Amiran Gogoladze def. Liam Etebar Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–26) 3 5:00 [d]
Light Heavyweight Marc Doussis def. Daniel Toledo Canellas Submission (guillotine choke) 1 2:59
Catchweight (150 lb) Ahmed Vila def. Kimbo Tinghaugen Submission (rear-naked choke) 1 3:55
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Middleweight Matěj Daněk def. Endrit Brajshori Submission (rear-naked choke) 2 3:26
  1. ^ For the Oktagon Middleweight Championship.
  2. ^ Oktagon Welterweight Tournament Quarterfinal; Lehoré missed weight (173.5 lb).
  3. ^ Oktagon Welterweight Tournament Quarterfinal.
  4. ^ Oktagon Welterweight Tournament Reserve bout.

An interim Oktagon Light Heavyweight Championship bout between Pavol Langer and Alexander Poppeck headlined the event.

Oktagon 45 Special: Mågård vs. Lima

[edit]
Oktagon 45 Special: Mågård vs. Lima
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateJuly 28, 2023
VenueCentral Tennis Court Štvanice
CityPrague, Czech Republic

Oktagon 45 Special: Mågård vs. Lima was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on July 28, 2023, in Prague, Czech Republic.

Background

[edit]

A Oktagon Bantamweight Championship bout between current champion Jonas Mågård and former KSW Bantamweight Champion Antun Račić was scheduled to headline the event.[6] However, Račić withdrew for unknown reasons and was replaced by Felipe Lima.[7]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Bantamweight Felipe Lima def. Jonas Mågård (c) Decision (unanimous) (48–45, 48–45, 48–46) 5 5:00 [a]
Middleweight Robert Bryczek def. Samuel Krištofič TKO (punches) 1 0:55 [b]
Featherweight Vladimír Lengál def. Michael Deiga-Scheck KO (knee) 1 2:59
Welterweight Ion Surdu def. Máté Kertész Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Lightweight Shem Rock def. Jan Malach Submission (rear-naked choke) 1 2:08
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Catchweight (176 lb) Radovan Úškrt def. Tato Primera TKO (doctor stoppage) 2 4:55
Middleweight Ole Magnor def. Václav Mikulášek Submission (rear-naked choke) 1 0:55
Middleweight Jamie Cordero def. Gabriel Török KO (head kick) 1 0:22
Featherweight Radek Roušal def. Armand Herczeg Decision (split) (30–27, 28–29, 29–28) 3 5:00
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Women's Bantamweight Róża Gumienna def. Lucia Krajčovič Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 29–28) 3 5:00
  1. ^ For the Oktagon Bantamweight Championship. Lima was deducted one point in round 4 due to an illegal knees.
  2. ^ Oktagon Middleweight title eliminator.

Oktagon 45: Sanikidze vs. Keita was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on July 29, 2023, in Prague, Czech Republic.

[edit]

A Oktagon Featherweight Championship unification bout between current champion Mate Sanikidze and interim champion (also a current Oktagon Lightweight Champion) Losene Keita headlined the event.[7]

PromotionOktagon MMA

Oktagon 47: Vémola vs. Langer was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on October 7, 2023, in Bratislava, Slovakia.

Background

[edit]

A Oktagon Light Heavyweight Championship unification bout between current champion (also former Oktagon Middleweight Champion) Karlos Vémola and interim champion Pavol Langer headlined the event.[9]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Light Heavyweight Karlos Vémola (c) def. Pavol Langer (ic) KO (punches) 1 0:07 [a]
Lightweight Ivan Buchinger def. Jakub Dohnal TKO (punches) 2 4:46

  5. ^ Sami missed weight (187 lb).

Oktagon 48: Aby vs. Garcia

[edit]
Oktagon 48: Aby vs. Garcia
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateNovember 4, 2023
VenueAO Arena
CityManchester, England

Oktagon 48: Aby vs. Garcia was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on November 4, 2023, in Manchester, England.

Background

[edit]

The event was headlined by an inaugural Oktagon Flyweight Championship bout between Aaron Aby and Elias Garcia.[10]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Flyweight Elias Garcia def. Aaron Aby TKO (doctor stoppage) 2 5:00 [a]
Catchweight (204 lb) Scott Askham def. Marc Doussis Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 30–27) 3 5:00
Lightweight Jakub Bahník vs. Akonne Wanliss No Contest (accidental knee to groin) 1 0:25 [b]
Middleweight Jake Quickenden def. Paul Smith TKO (punches) 1 1:16 [c]
Bantamweight Jack Cartwright def. Júnior Assis Submission (scarf hold armlock) 1 2:15
Lightweight George Staines def. Denis Frimpong Submission (guillotine choke) 4 2:02 [d]
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Welterweight Ion Surdu def. Alex Lehoré KO (punches) 1 4:10
Catchweight (150 lb) Shoaib Yousaf def. Konmon Deh Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 30–27) 3 5:00
Catchweight (190 lb) Zdeněk Polívka def. Lee Chadwick Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Lightweight Hascen Neri Gelezi def. Aaron McDonald Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
  1. ^ For the inaugural Oktagon Flyweight Championship.
  2. ^ Accidental knee to the groin rendered Bahník unable to continue.
  3. ^ Oktagon's UK reality show: Stage to the Cage Final.
  4. ^ Oktagon Challenge: England vs. Ireland Tournament Final.

Oktagon 49: Moeil vs. Todev

[edit]
Oktagon 49: Moeil vs. Todev
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateNovember 18, 2023
VenueLanxess Arena
CityCologne, Germany

Oktagon 49: Moeil vs. Todev was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on November 18, 2023, in Cologne, Germany.

Background

[edit]

A Oktagon Heavyweight Championship bout for the vacant title between Hatef Moeil and Lazar Todev headlined the event.[11]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Heavyweight Hatef Moeil def. Lazar Todev Decision (unanimous) (49–46, 49–46, 48–47) 5 5:00 [a]
Catchweight (176 lb) Leandro Silva def. Christian Eckerlin Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Middleweight Kerim Engizek def. Adam Horváth TKO (punches) 1 3:48
Featherweight Corey Fry def. Deniz Ilbay Submission (armbar) 1 3:35 [b]
Welterweight Niklas Stolze def. Andrej Kalašnik KO (punches) 1 3:40
Middleweight Matthew Bonner def. Cihad Akipa Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Lightweight Konrad Dyrschka def. Thiago Silva Decision (majority) (28–28, 28–27, 29–27) 3 5:00
Light Heavyweight Jorick Montagnac def. Steve MacDonald KO (punches) 1 4:10
Catchweight (150 lb) Arijan Topallaj def. Roman Paulus Submission (brabo choke) 1 1:40 [c]
Featherweight Jake McHugh def. Ivan Klevets Technical Submission (triangle choke) 2 2:32
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Welterweight Lukáš Eliáš def. Finn Griessmann KO (punches) 1 2:39
  1. ^ For the vacant Oktagon Heavyweight Championship.
  2. ^ Ilbay missed weight (148 lb).
  3. ^ Topallaj missed weight (156.3 lb).

Oktagon 50: Keita vs. Samsonidse

[edit]
Oktagon 50: Keita vs. Samsonidse
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateDecember 9, 2023
VenueOstravar Aréna
CityOstrava, Czech Republic

Oktagon 50: Keita vs. Samsonidse was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on December 9, 2023, in Ostrava, Czech Republic.

Background

[edit]

A Oktagon Featherweight Championship for the vacant title between former Oktagon Lightweight Champion Losene Keita and Niko Samsonidse headlined the event. Former champion Mate Sanikidze announced that he vacated the title when he moved down to bantamweight.[12]

A Oktagon Lightweight Championship rematch for the vacant title between Ronald Paradeiser and former champion (also former Oktagon Featherweight Champion) Ivan Buchinger served as co-main event. The pairing previously met at Oktagon 27 in February 2021, with Buchinger captured the title via first round submission.[13]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Featherweight Losene Keita def. Niko Samsonidse KO (punches) 2 0:23 [a]
Lightweight Ronald Paradeiser def. Ivan Buchinger TKO (doctor stoppage) 3 5:00 [b]
Featherweight Samuel Bark def. Vladimír Lengál Decision (split) (29–28, 28–29, 29–28) 3 5:00
Middleweight Matěj Peňáz def. Ole Magnor KO (punches) 1 0:29
Lightweight Stefano Catacoli def. Jan Široký Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–27) 3 5:00
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Light Heavyweight Rafael Xavier def. Dan Vinni TKO (punches) 2 1:27
Lightweight Ebrahim Hosseinpour def. Matěj Kuzník Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Welterweight Máté Kertész def. Ammari Diedrick Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Catchweight (196 lb) Ondřej Raška def. Michal Kotalík TKO (foot injury) 2 0:28 [c]
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Featherweight Marco Novák def. Michal Konrád TKO (punches and elbows) 2 4:07
  1. ^ For the vacant Oktagon Featherweight Championship.
  2. ^ For the vacant Oktagon Lightweight Championship.
  3. ^ Kotalík missed weight (197.5 lb).

Oktagon 51: Tipsport Gamechanger Final

[edit]
Oktagon 51: Tipsport Gamechanger Final
PromotionOktagon MMA
DateDecember 29, 2023
VenueO2 Arena
CityPrague, Czech Republic

Oktagon 51: Tipsport Gamechanger Final was a mixed martial arts event held by Oktagon MMA on December 29, 2023, in Prague, Czech Republic.

Background

[edit]

The Tipsport Gamechanger 1 welterweight tournament final between Bojan Veličković and Andreas Michailidis headlined the event. While, an interim Oktagon Middleweight Championship bout between Vlasto Čepo and Piotr Wawrzyniak served as the co-main event.[14]

Results

[edit]
Main card (DAZN)
Weight class Method Round Time Notes
Welterweight Bojan Veličković def. Andreas Michailidis Submission (rear-naked choke) 3 3:21 [a]
Middleweight Piotr Wawrzyniak def. Vlasto Čepo TKO (punches) 3 3:19 [b]
Catchweight (195 lb) Samuel Krištofič def. Karlos Vémola KO (punch) 1 1:48
Light Heavyweight Daniel Škvor def. Martin Zawada Decision (unanimous) (30–27, 30–27, 30–27) 3 5:00
Welterweight Stefano Paternò def. Ion Surdu Submission (armbar) 2 2:37 [c]
Preliminary card (DAZN)
Catchweight (150 lb) Karol Ryšavý def. Henrique Madureira TKO (punches) 1 1:59
Women's Strawweight Eva Dourthe def. Chiara Penco Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00 [d]
Featherweight Radek Roušal def. Kallum Parker Decision (unanimous) (29–28, 29–28, 29–28) 3 5:00
Catchweight (121 lb) Mallory Martin def. Magdaléna Šormová Decision (split) (29–28, 28–29, 29–28) 3 5:00
Free Prelims (YouTube)
Lightweight Hassan Shaaban def. Tomáš Fiala KO (flying knee) 1 1:58 [e]
  1. ^ Oktagon Welterweight Tournament Final.
  2. ^ For the interim Oktagon Middleweight Championship.
  3. ^ Surdu missed weight (172 lb).
  4. ^ Oktagon Women's Strawweight title eliminator.
  5. ^ Shaaban missed weight (157.6 lb).
